title: Epsilon Characterization of Supremum
statement: Let $A\subseteq\mathbb{R}$ and let $s\in\mathbb{R}$. The element $s$ satisfies the \emph{epsilon characterization of the supremum} of $A$ if and only if $s$ is an upper bound of $A$ and for every $\varepsilon>0$ there exists $a\in A$ such that $s-\varepsilon<a$.
